title: 2026 NBA Free Agency Grades: Lakers, 76ers Shine, Celtics Struggle
body:
The 2026 NBA free agency period has been nothing short of explosive, with a flurry of blockbuster trades and signings sending shockwaves throughout the league. Just days into the offseason, teams are already making bold moves to reshape their rosters and contend for a championship. The Los Angeles Lakers and Philadelphia 76ers have been particularly aggressive, earning high marks for their savvy acquisitions and strategic maneuvering. Meanwhile, other teams, such as the Boston Celtics, are facing scrutiny for their underwhelming performances in the early going.

One of the most stunning developments has been the exodus of high-profile players to new destinations. The sudden departure of Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors has left many observers stunned, as the former NBA champion and Finals MVP joins a talented young core in the Great White North. Similarly, the 76ers' acquisition of Jaylen Brown has bolstered their already-formidable lineup, positioning them as a legitimate title contender in the Eastern Conference. These moves have significant implications for the balance of power in the league, as teams that were once considered also-rans are now vaulting into the conversation as potential champions.

The Lakers, in particular, have been lauded for their shrewd decision-making, having assembled a deep and talented roster through a combination of draft picks, trades, and free agent signings. By addressing key positional needs and bolstering their bench, the Lakers have positioned themselves for a deep playoff run, and possibly even a championship. The 76ers, meanwhile, have demonstrated a keen understanding of their roster's strengths and weaknesses, making targeted additions that complement their existing core. These moves have earned both teams high grades in the early going, as they appear poised to make significant noise in the upcoming season.

Not all teams have been so fortunate, however. The Celtics, once considered a perennial powerhouse, are facing questions about their offseason strategy. Despite having a talented young core, the team has been relatively quiet in free agency, failing to make the kind of splashy additions that might have propelled them to the top of the Eastern Conference. As a result, the Celtics are facing criticism for their lack of aggressiveness, with some observers wondering if they have the necessary pieces to compete with the likes of the 76ers and Lakers.

As the free agency period continues to unfold, it will be fascinating to see how the rest of the league responds to the early movers. Will other teams follow the lead of the Lakers and 76ers, or will they take a more cautious approach? The answers to these questions will have significant implications for the upcoming season, as teams jockey for position in the ever-competitive NBA landscape. With the draft and free agency still in full swing, there is plenty of time for teams to make their mark and reshape their rosters.

As the dust settles on the 2026 NBA free agency period, one thing is clear: the landscape of the league has been forever altered. With blockbuster trades and signings continuing to reshape the balance of power, fans are in for a thrilling ride as the new season approaches. As teams continue to maneuver and adjust their rosters, the stage is set for a wide-open and unpredictable campaign, with multiple contenders vying for supremacy. With the Lakers and 76ers setting the pace, the rest of the league will be forced to keep up – or risk being left behind.
